description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages setup, test, build, and deploy and define the variables APP_NAME set to notset, JEKYLL_ENV set to production, and NODE_ENV set to test. In the setup stage add a prepare job that runs `echo 'Prepare jobs'`. In the test stage add a test:jekyll job that runs `cd UI-coming-soon`, `bundle install`, and `bundle exec jekyll build -d test`, uploads the UI-coming-soon/test directory as an artifact, and is excluded from the master branch; also add a test:node job that runs `echo 'Run tests job'`. In the build stage add three jobs—build-api-image, build-service-image, and build-ui-image—each using the docker:latest image with the docker:dind service, executing `docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p "$CI_BUILD_TOKEN" "$CI_REGISTRY"`, changing into their respective directories (ask-jocko-api, twit-service, UI-node), building an image tagged `$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/<name>:$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME` with `docker build -t "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/<name>:$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME" .` and pushing it with `docker push "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/<name>:$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME"`. In the deploy stage add a pages job using the starefossen/ruby-node:latest image that creates a public folder, runs `bundle install` and `bundle exec jekyll build -d public` inside UI-coming-soon, moves the built files to the public directory, publishes the public folder as a Pages artifact, and runs only on the master branch; also add a start-deploy job that runs `echo 'Deploy started'` and sets the variable GIT_CHECKOUT to false.
